behaviors.description: The embedding vector produced for a given query string has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at the model-output boundary: the embedding provider can return a different dimension (e.g., model/version drift), which a stub would hide. This behavior depends on a real AI/ML output property (vector length), so Integration is too low. Workflow is not required because there is only one model decision point.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real embedding model configured for production with a fixed query, then assert returned vector length equals the live index dimension retrieved from vector DB metadata. Fail on any mismatch.

behaviors.description: The similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic retrieval/ranking logic across service + vector-store modules. A real LLM is not involved, and no real OS/network/subprocess behavior is necessary if run against a local/in-process vector store fixture. Rejected System because behavior text is about ordering correctness, not real network/OS failure modes.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a local test index with known vectors and run similarity search through the service layer; assert returned scores are monotonically non-increasing and IDs match expected cosine ranking.

behaviors.description: The LLM re-ranking step changes the order of at least some results compared to the raw similarity ranking when the query has nuanced intent.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly evaluates model-driven ranking relevance for nuanced intent. A mock reranker would trivially pass/fail without testing real model judgment, so mocking hides the real failure. Rejected Workflow because only one agentic decision (the rerank call) is being validated, not a multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Provide a curated query + candidate set where lexical similarity conflicts with contextual intent; invoke the real reranker and assert output order differs from raw similarity order in expected direction on repeated trials with acceptance threshold.

behaviors.description: A query with no close matches in the database returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure boundary is handoff between similarity scores and application threshold/filter logic. This can be caught with deterministic module wiring and controlled index data. Rejected Agentic because behavior text is about 'no close matches in the database' and empty-set gating, not model-output quality; reranker is irrelevant when candidate set is empty.
behaviors.test_description: Load a local index with domain-specific vectors, issue an out-of-domain query through API/service path, and assert zero candidates survive similarity-threshold filtering (empty response payload).

behaviors.description: The end-to-end latency from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds for the median case.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end performance SLO across real HTTP/API, embedding call, vector DB query, and rerank call. In-process fakes hide true latency distribution and tail effects. Rejected Agentic because assertion is purely latency infrastructure (not output-quality correctness), which is explicitly a non-agentic orchestration/perf check.
behaviors.test_description: Run a representative load test against the deployed API path using real dependencies, record end-to-end response times, and assert median latency (p50) remains <2.0s over a statistically meaningful sample window.
